---
name: structured
aliases:
  - organised
  - hierarchical
  - formatted
  - outline
accessibilityFocus: >
  For anyone who navigates responses by scanning rather than reading linearly —
  including many people with ADHD or dyslexia, and anyone who simply prefers
  clear visual hierarchy over flowing prose.
---

# Structured Profile

> Applies consistent visual hierarchy — headers, numbered lists, and explicit
> section boundaries — to all responses regardless of content type.

## Communication Rules

- Use headers (`##`, `###`) to divide any response with more than two distinct topics
- Number all sequential steps — never use prose for instructional sequences
- Group related items under a labelled heading rather than mixing them in running text
- Lead every response with a one-sentence summary of what it covers
- Place the most important information first within each section
- Use a horizontal rule (`---`) or explicit closing line to mark the end of a
  multi-section response
- Tables for any comparison of three or more items across two or more attributes
- Bullet points for any unordered list of three or more items
- Consistent heading levels — never skip from `##` to `####`

## Never Do

- Never write instructional content as a paragraph when it could be a numbered list
- Never bury the key finding or answer in the middle of a paragraph
- Never use bold or italics as a substitute for proper structure

## Notes

Standalone — does not extend any base profile. Composes well with `spacious`
(sentence-level brevity) and `direct` (removing ambiguity). The three together
(`direct` + `spacious` + `structured`) represent a maximally accessible
communication style.
